metadata.title: Why a schema enters as types, travels as text, and returns as types
metadata.topic: Round-trip type safety through structured-output parsing
metadata.purpose: The SDK encodes the Zod schema to a JSON Schema constraint outbound, Claude returns a conformant JSON string, then the SDK validates that string back through the SAME in-memory Zod object — recovering both runtime value and static type. The schema is touched at both ends of the arc.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro af_kore) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.still_lanes: c2v (ring pipeline, schema reused at both ends)
metadata.prerequisites: TS type erasure, JSON Schema basics, Zod
metadata.exclusions: jsonSchemaOutputFormat differences, streaming structured outputs, validation-error recovery
metadata.visual_object: A ring-shaped pipeline: Zod schema → JSON Schema (out) → JSON string (back) → Zod `.parse()` → typed object, same node at both ends.
metadata.hook: TypeScript types vanish at runtime and Claude only emits plain text, yet `client.messages.parse()` hands back a fully typed object in the exact shape you declared — with no validation code from you.

beats.narration_text: Pass a Zod schema to `zodOutputFormat`, call `.parse()`, read `.parsed_output` as a typed object — though the wire only ever carried a JSON Schema out and a JSON string back.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: The schema is erased on the wire and Claude knows nothing of TypeScript — so how does the typed `parsed_output` reappear with the right shape on the return path?
TypeScript types vanish at runtime and Claude only emits plain text, yet `client.messages.parse()` hands back a fully typed object in the exact shape you declared — with no validation code from you.

The SDK encodes the Zod schema to a JSON Schema constraint outbound, Claude returns a conformant JSON string, then the SDK validates that string back through the SAME in-memory Zod object — recovering both runtime value and static type. The schema is touched at both ends of the arc.
beats.visual: A ring-shaped pipeline: Zod schema → JSON Schema (out) → JSON string (back) → Zod `.parse()` → typed object, same node at both ends.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: `z.object({primes: z.array(z.number())})` → JSON Schema in the request → Claude returns `{"primes":[2,3,5]}` → Zod validates → caller gets `{primes:[2,3,5]}` typed `number[]`.

The SDK encodes the Zod schema to a JSON Schema constraint outbound, Claude returns a conformant JSON string, then the SDK validates that string back through the SAME in-memory Zod object — recovering both runtime value and static type. The schema is touched at both ends of the arc.
